title: Coordinate frames and the transform tree
description: Every pose is meaningful only relative to a frame; tf2 maintains a time-stamped tree so data can be transformed between frames at the right moment.
body: Use when interpreting any pose, sensor reading, or command. A pose has no meaning without a frame (base_link, odom, map, sensor frames), and tf2 stores the time-varying rigid transforms between frames as a tree so any frame can be reached from any other at a given time. Transforms are time-stamped because the robot moves; using a transform from the wrong time misplaces the data. Do not assume two readings share a frame or that a transform is constant. Actions: stamp data with frame and time and always transform at the data's timestamp. Validation signals include a connected tree and correct ground-truth transforms. Risk boundary: a frame or time mismatch sends commands to the wrong physical location; verify both before acting on transformed data.
